title: Thresholded concurrency hedging to stabilize initial-token latency across multi-variant workloads
preview: Several enterprise customers across Hosted and Dedicated reported intermittent 200-600ms TTFT spikes and tokens/sec drop when model-variant fanout and tenant concurrency peak simultaneously. Investigation shows contention at the serving queue boundary combined with aggressive worker multiplexing: short-lived, high-p...
text: Several enterprise customers across Hosted and Dedicated reported intermittent 200-600ms TTFT spikes and tokens/sec drop when model-variant fanout and tenant concurrency peak simultaneously. Investigation shows contention at the serving queue boundary combined with aggressive worker multiplexing: short-lived, high-priority requests preempt kernel scheduling and force warmstart/eviction churn for other inflight streams. Result: first-token latency spikes and sustained throughput collapse for some tenants and private deployments. This ticket implements a defensible, low-risk hedging layer that thresholds concurrency per tenant/model-variant and issues polite hedged requests (soft duplicate to warm pool) to avoid blocking kernel scheduling and reduce eviction storms. The goal is to regain median TTFT and stabilize tokens/sec within SLA while preserving throughput for bulk workloads.

title: Prompt Sentinel Matrix, Regression Severity Tiers, and Staged Rollback Choreography for Optimize v2
preview: Purpose: Define a production-ready quality gating and rollback choreography for Optimize model rollouts. This ticket captures the PRD-level requirements for prompt sentinel composition, multi-tier regression severity rules, A/B criteria for canaries, and the automated/manual actions that should occur when signals cr...
text:
Purpose: Define a production-ready quality gating and rollback choreography for Optimize model rollouts. This ticket captures the PRD-level requirements for prompt sentinel composition, multi-tier regression severity rules, A/B criteria for canaries, and the automated/manual actions that should occur when signals cross thresholds. The goal is to make rollouts safe and actionable while minimizing unnecessary rollbacks and customer noise.

Background: Recent experiments with quantized model variants and batching changes produced subtle downstream quality regressions in targeted cohorts. Current guardrails are ad-hoc (manual dashboards and pager alerts) and do not codify graduated responses. This PRD standardizes signal definitions and explicit remediation steps so Product, SRE, and Runtime can automate first-line mitigations and surface only actionable incidents to on-call.

Scope:
- De...

title: API Fidelity PRD for Generation, Embeddings, and Reranking — Launch Rundown & Acceptance Matrix
preview: Purpose: Define a concise behavioral and operational spec that guarantees fidelity of the hosted API across generation (chat/completions), embeddings, and reranking surfaces. This PRD focuses on exact behavior contracts, compatibility tolerances, and an operational launch rundown with an acceptance matrix to minimiz...
text: Purpose: Define a concise behavioral and operational spec that guarantees fidelity of the hosted API across generation (chat/completions), embeddings, and reranking surfaces. This PRD focuses on exact behavior contracts, compatibility tolerances, and an operational launch rundown with an acceptance matrix to minimize customer regressions. It clarifies scope changes since the Q1 spec, captures trade-offs (latency vs. unit cost vs. determinism), and lists the rollout playbook for staged region + tier canary + automatic fallback.

title: Catalog launch ops lane and stakeholder guardrails for new model additions
preview: Objective: Define a repeatable, low-friction operational lane for getting new model additions from intake -> catalog listing -> public announcement. This ticket captures a granular checklist, stakeholder responsibilities, release-note drafting cadence, and guarded rollout gates for quality and telemetry.
text: Objective: Define a repeatable, low-friction operational lane for getting new model additions from intake -> catalog listing -> public announcement. This ticket captures a granular checklist, stakeholder responsibilities, release-note drafting cadence, and guarded rollout gates for quality and telemetry.

title: Quantization signature stability harness for embedding models (INT8/FP16)
preview: Create a reusable stability harness that generates deterministic quantization signatures for embedding models (INT8 and FP16 paths), runs calibration and post-quantization validation, and surfaces cosine-similarity drift regressions over time. The goal is to enable safe rollouts of quantized embedding variants into ...
text: Create a reusable stability harness that generates deterministic quantization signatures for embedding models (INT8 and FP16 paths), runs calibration and post-quantization validation, and surfaces cosine-similarity drift regressions over time. The goal is to enable safe rollouts of quantized embedding variants into Dedicated and Private deployments by enforcing automated acceptance gates and clear rollback criteria.

title: Stratified prompt sampling and actionable regression contracts for Optimize evals
preview: Define a reproducible, actionable evaluation contract for Optimize that ties prompt-set sampling, regression thresholds, and A/B decision rules to concrete rollout/rollback actions. The objective is to make eval outputs prescriptive (what to do) rather than descriptive (what happened). This ticket consolidates requi...
text: Define a reproducible, actionable evaluation contract for Optimize that ties prompt-set sampling, regression thresholds, and A/B decision rules to concrete rollout/rollback actions. The objective is to make eval outputs prescriptive (what to do) rather than descriptive (what happened). This ticket consolidates requirements for sampling stratification, evidence thresholds, stop/rollback logic, monitoring signals, and handoff to SRE/eng for automations.

title: Function-call JSON stability harness for post‑training INT8/FP8 quant & calibration replay
preview: Build a focused stability and regression harness that validates function-call / JSON schema adherence after post-training quantization (INT8 and FP8) using calibration replay artifacts. The goal is to measure token-level divergence and schema drift across model variants and calibration checkpoints and automate detec...
text: Build a focused stability and regression harness that validates function-call / JSON schema adherence after post-training quantization (INT8 and FP8) using calibration replay artifacts. The goal is to measure token-level divergence and schema drift across model variants and calibration checkpoints and automate detection of regressions that would break structured output consumers.

title: Conversation framing sanitizer and token-stream alignment for pinned model rollouts
preview: Summary: New model bundles arriving from third-party contributors include inconsistent conversation framing (role delimiters, invisible separators, and control tokens) that leads to subtle tokenization mismatches when we pin model versions and publish performance profiles. This ticket implements a conservative 'conv...
text: Summary: New model bundles arriving from third-party contributors include inconsistent conversation framing (role delimiters, invisible separators, and control tokens) that leads to subtle tokenization mismatches when we pin model versions and publish performance profiles. This ticket implements a conservative 'conversation framing sanitizer' in the onboarding pipeline that normalizes role markers, strips invisible separators, and produces a canonical token-stream preview used to validate performance profile publishing and runtime pinning. Motivation: Without normalization, customers see token-level regressions after pinning (token counts differ from onboarding benchmark), and the profile publish job can publish misleading per-token latency/cost metrics. Goal: ensure the onboarding preview and published profile match the runtime tokenization the serving stack will produce for pinned m...

title: Staged Pin Eviction & Regional Variant Escalation with Observability Hooks
preview: Goal: design and implement a staged eviction and fallback mechanism for model version pins that minimizes customer disruption during deprecations or regional capacity disruptions. This ticket covers: 1) pin metadata TTL and staged eviction windows, 2) a priority escalation path for regional/model-variant fallback, 3...
text: Goal: design and implement a staged eviction and fallback mechanism for model version pins that minimizes customer disruption during deprecations or regional capacity disruptions. This ticket covers: 1) pin metadata TTL and staged eviction windows, 2) a priority escalation path for regional/model-variant fallback, 3) telemetry hooks and SLO signals that drive automated grace windows, and 4) compatibility checks for variant fallbacks (quantization/hardware compatibility enforcement). Success criteria: (a) zero user-visible 5xxs during a controlled pin eviction across 3 customers in staging, (b) bounded tail latency increase <= 15% for affected requests during an eviction grace window, and (c) clear rollback path and audit trail for every automated fallback. Non-goals: making permanent changes to pricing, or modifying model catalog discovery flows beyond adding compatibility metadata fi...

title: Per-language repair playbook for SDK errors and streaming recovery
preview: Problem: SDK consumers struggle to map low-level runtime failures to concrete remediation steps. Streaming operations compound the issue because partial progress is often lost and client authors are uncertain when to resume, retry, or fallback. This ticket delivers a per-language (Python/TypeScript/Go) "repair playb...
text: Problem: SDK consumers struggle to map low-level runtime failures to concrete remediation steps. Streaming operations compound the issue because partial progress is often lost and client authors are uncertain when to resume, retry, or fallback. This ticket delivers a per-language (Python/TypeScript/Go) "repair playbook": standardized error envelope, stable error codes, short human-readable recovery blurb, and language-specific quick-fix snippets for common failure classes (transient network, upstream model OOM, auth/KMS failures, and stream checkpoint/resume hints). The goal is consistent developer UX across SDKs and measurable reduction in support tickets and mean time to recovery for production incidents.

title: TS SDK: checkpointed streaming adapter for graceful reconnect and interop
preview: Implement a TypeScript SDK streaming adapter that attaches lightweight checkpoint markers to streamed payloads so clients can gracefully reconnect and resume consumption across transient network failures while maintaining compatibility with both AsyncIterator and Web Streams consumer patterns.
text: Implement a TypeScript SDK streaming adapter that attaches lightweight checkpoint markers to streamed payloads so clients can gracefully reconnect and resume consumption across transient network failures while maintaining compatibility with both AsyncIterator and Web Streams consumer patterns.

text: Create an 'Evidence Harvest Bridge' service and UX surface that maps compliance controls to automated collectors, enforces territorial data residency and retention rules, and produces immutable artifact bundles for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 evidence. This work ties the control catalog, collector registry, and retention policy engine into a single orchestrated flow so customers can request exportable compliance packages scoped by region, control-set, and time range.

title: Directional token buckets + streaming latency surcharge experiment

title: Fallback priority graph and staggered threshold calibrator
preview: Problem: We need a deterministic, testable mechanism to decide when to trip per-dependency / per-model / per-tenant circuits and how to pick safe fallbacks. The existing heuristics are ad-hoc (static error thresholds + simple decay) and have produced either noisy trips for low-traffic tenants or slow reactions to ca...
text:
Problem: We need a deterministic, testable mechanism to decide when to trip per-dependency / per-model / per-tenant circuits and how to pick safe fallbacks. The existing heuristics are ad-hoc (static error thresholds + simple decay) and have produced either noisy trips for low-traffic tenants or slow reactions to cascading latency spikes in multi-region failover scenarios.

Goal: Build a calibration and orchestration artifact that: 1) generates a prioritized graph of fallback destinations for each (tenant, model, region) tuple (eg: same-model-quantized, smaller-model, cached-prefix, no-op/400), 2) computes staggered trip thresholds that account for traffic volume and downstream dependency criticality, and 3) provides a simulation harness to validate threshold choices against historical traffic and injected failures so we can pick thresholds that minimize SLO breaches while bounding co...

title: Dedicated cluster post-incident: allocator tuning, noisy-neighbor attribution, and burst smoothing
preview: Summary: A dedicated-cluster capacity event led to elevated evictions and degraded tail latency for multiple tenants during a traffic surge on 2026-02-09. Root cause analysis shows a confluence of (1) an unexpectedly noisy tenant with sustained KV/prefix cache pressure, (2) reservation mis-sizing for a subset of poo...
text: Summary: A dedicated-cluster capacity event led to elevated evictions and degraded tail latency for multiple tenants during a traffic surge on 2026-02-09. Root cause analysis shows a confluence of (1) an unexpectedly noisy tenant with sustained KV/prefix cache pressure, (2) reservation mis-sizing for a subset of pools (under-provisioned headroom), and (3) burst token logic that allowed short-term oversubscription without adequate backoff. The incident surfaced limits in our allocator heuristics and insufficient attribution telemetry to quickly identify the noisy workload. This ticket tracks the post-incident action items: allocator tuning, noisy-neighbor attribution, reservation reconciliation, burst smoothing, and improved alerting/playbooks.

text:
Goal: reduce time-to-first-success for AWS/GCP Marketplace buyers by instrumenting the end-to-end onboarding funnel and identifying drop-offs.

We need consistent, debuggable telemetry across:
1) entitlement lookup latency + outcomes
2) org linking success/failure (including existing org edge cases)
3) API key creation completion
4) first successful API request after provisioning

This ticket adds event definitions, implementation hooks (console + backend), and the first dashboards/alerts so GTM/Eng can monitor launch readiness and regressions.

Primary questions we want to answer:
- Where are users dropping? (entitlement check vs org link vs API key vs first request)
- How long does entitlement verification take by provider (AWS vs GCP) and by error type?
- How often do entitlement propagation delays happen vs true configuration errors?
- What’s median and p95 time from subscription ...

title: API Stability Contract & Client Migration Timeline for Legacy Generate/Stream Endpoints
preview: Executive summary: This PRD defines the contract and timeline for phasing out the legacy /v0/generate and multiplexed streaming endpoints and guiding clients to the unified inference API. The deliverable is a prescriptive migration timeline, stability guarantees for each phase (SLO windows and compatibility guarante...
text: Executive summary: This PRD defines the contract and timeline for phasing out the legacy /v0/generate and multiplexed streaming endpoints and guiding clients to the unified inference API. The deliverable is a prescriptive migration timeline, stability guarantees for each phase (SLO windows and compatibility guarantees), clear acceptance criteria for SDKs and server behavior, and a customer-facing migration playbook. The output will enable predictable client upgrades while protecting production traffic via circuit breakers, telemetry gates, and rollback paths.

title: Anchor-based contrast suite and ranked-pair concordance eval for embedding+reranker
preview: Build an offline evaluation harness that ties config changes and model swaps to concrete embedding + reranker quality signals. The suite will: (1) construct anchor queries from production cohorts, (2) generate hard negatives and synthetic paraphrase negatives, (3) run the embedding + reranker pipeline to produce ran...
text: Build an offline evaluation harness that ties config changes and model swaps to concrete embedding + reranker quality signals. The suite will: (1) construct anchor queries from production cohorts, (2) generate hard negatives and synthetic paraphrase negatives, (3) run the embedding + reranker pipeline to produce ranked lists, and (4) compute a small, interpretable metric set including recall@K, precision@K, MRR, ranked-pair concordance, and embedding-space shift scores. The goal is to produce a delta report for every config/model change that can be used to gate rollouts and trigger automated investigations. The harness should be fast enough for nightly pre-merge runs on sampled cohorts and robust enough to run as part of CI for significant config PRs.

text:
## Context
We’re rolling out the first NA pilot of the PLG-to-sales signal routing pipeline + playbook. This ticket defines *who* gets included, *what guardrails* prevent spam/false positives, *how we measure impact*, and a *weekly review cadence* with Sales + Growth.

Pilot goals:
- Increase conversion from self-serve → sales-assisted evaluation (Dedicated/Private) without degrading customer trust.
- Validate signal thresholds + suppression rules (noise reduction) before expanding to other regions.
- Prove we can attribute outcomes back to signals (meeting booked, eval started, opp created).

Non-goals (pilot):
- Fully automated outbound sequences with no human review.
- Expanding beyond NA ownership/territories.

## Pilot scope (NA)
**Duration:** 3–4 weeks initial run, then decide expand/iterate.

**Sales coverage:** NA AEs + SE involvement on specific signals (see guardrails).

title: Governance request orchestration for granular log redaction and export scenarios
preview: Create an orchestration and decision framework that intake, scores, and routes customer requests for log redaction, retention adjustments, PII handling, and audit exports. The goal is to reduce time-to-response for enterprise customers while ensuring every request follows the right legal/compliance path, required te...
text: Create an orchestration and decision framework that intake, scores, and routes customer requests for log redaction, retention adjustments, PII handling, and audit exports. The goal is to reduce time-to-response for enterprise customers while ensuring every request follows the right legal/compliance path, required tech work, and SLOs. Deliverables: (1) triage scoring rubric and routing rules, (2) templated response flows for Support/CS/SEs, (3) engineering runbook stubs for implementation (API + console), and (4) intake automation design (Slack/Forms -> Linear -> gated escalations). Emphasis on minimizing manual review where safe (policy templates + automated redaction checks) while preserving legal hold and export fidelity when required.

text:
## Context
We are shipping a new in-Console onboarding checklist (“Guided onboarding + first production launch”) for Hosted API. This ticket defines:
- Rollout phases (internal dogfood → gradual cohort exposure → GA)
- Feature flag gating + cohort selection rules
- Kill switch criteria + operational monitoring
- Comms plan for Support/CS and internal stakeholders

Goal: de-risk launch and avoid creating new onboarding confusion or support burden while we validate step funnel instrumentation and checklist state correctness.

## Owners / stakeholders
- PM (Hosted API): Nikhil Sharma
- PM (Console): Marissa Cole
- Program sponsor: Alex Martinez
- Rollout + lifecycle messaging: Ben Carter (assignee)
- Console Eng: Dylan Brooks / Chloe Martin
- Telemetry: Logan Wright
- Support enablement: Dev Patel
- SRE review: Sean Gallagher

title: Routing Governor: interactive Console sandbox for region/tier selection and fallback variants
preview: Build an interactive 'Routing Governor' sandbox in the Redwood Console that lets customers author, validate, and preview routing policies that express region selection, tier routing, and tiered fallback model variants. This feature surfaces validation hints, live cost/latency estimates, and a simulated traffic previ...
text: Build an interactive 'Routing Governor' sandbox in the Redwood Console that lets customers author, validate, and preview routing policies that express region selection, tier routing, and tiered fallback model variants. This feature surfaces validation hints, live cost/latency estimates, and a simulated traffic preview so product/platform teams can safely iterate on policies before applying them.

title: Per-tenant Commitment: Virtual Credit Buckets and Late Adjustment Billing PRD
preview: Implement a per-tenant "virtual credit" commitment mechanism to allow customers to pre-allocate committed compute/usage in flexible buckets that roll forward, support late adjustments, and produce clear invoice line items. This PRD defines product requirements, acceptance criteria, API contracts, billing reconciliat...
text: Implement a per-tenant "virtual credit" commitment mechanism to allow customers to pre-allocate committed compute/usage in flexible buckets that roll forward, support late adjustments, and produce clear invoice line items. This PRD defines product requirements, acceptance criteria, API contracts, billing reconciliation, migration paths for existing committed plans, and rollout controls.

title: Refactor sampling policy to bounded-emission + low-overhead tail detection
preview: Deliver a refactor of the tracing sampling layer to enforce a bounded span-emission budget per service while adding a low-cost tail-detection heuristic. Goal is to reduce CPU + network overhead from high-cardinality trace traffic without losing high-fidelity traces for tail latency and error analysis. This change sh...
text: Deliver a refactor of the tracing sampling layer to enforce a bounded span-emission budget per service while adding a low-cost tail-detection heuristic. Goal is to reduce CPU + network overhead from high-cardinality trace traffic without losing high-fidelity traces for tail latency and error analysis. This change should be safe for hosted and dedicated deployments, support config overrides per-customer, and include rollout/rollback tooling in Console.
